sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
ஒய்யாரமாய் ஊஞ்சலாடும் ஓங்கார கணபதே
மெய்யார் உரைப்பார் ஐயனே நீ இணையிலா ஈசனென்றே
sections.translit:
oyyAramAy UnjalAdum OngkAra gaNapathE
meyyAr uraippAr aiyanE nI iNaiyilA IsanendRE
sections.en: Oh, Lord Ganapathi, moving gracefully on the swing, who will tell the truth: that you are an incomparable deity?

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
ஆடலில் பாடலில் ஆகம ஞானத்தில் அடியாரை
நாடலில் அவர்தம் அருவினை அகற்றலில்
sections.translit:
Adalil pAdalil Agama nyAnatthil adiyArai
nAdalil avartham aruvinai agatRalil
sections.en: You are unsurpassed in dance and music, your deep knowledge of scriptures, and in seeking out your devotees and removing their sins, however difficult that may be.

sections.label: Charanam
sections.ta:
தந்தை தாய் நீயே தயாநிதே ஏங்கிடுமெனை
தந்திரமேதும் புரியாமல் வந்தாள் வெண்ணிற
தந்தமதை தரித்த வேழமுக வேந்தே சாபம்
தந்துமதை மறந்து பிறை சூடிய பெருமானே
sections.translit:
thandhai thAi nIyE dhayAnithE Engidumenai
thandhiramEdhum puriyAmal vandhAL veNNiRa
thandhamadhai dharittha vEzhamuga vEndhE sAbam
thandhumadhai maRandhu piRai sUdiya perumAnE
sections.en: Oh, compassion incarnate, elephant-faced king of all gods with a white tusk, you are both mother and father to me. Please come without any tricks and be my king; you initially cursed the moon, and then in your extreme soft heart you finally pardoned and adorned the moon despite his vile behavior. You stand out among the gods. Author’s Note This song was written after a visit to the residence of friends Shantha and Sundararaman in Los Altos, CA. There was a radiant metallic idol of Ganesha seated on a shiny swing or oonjal. Shantha insisted that the poet should write a song on the divinity in her house. The wordplay in the charanam of this song is based on a similar one in the Kannada raga kriti of Saint Thyagaraja beginning with Saketaniketana.
